assessment_phase: Tier-1 Readiness Assessment (NOT a conformance claim)

out_of_scope: deterministic SAST scanners (aegis scan / aegis audit)

requirements.rationale: Machine-readable RoE schema (Zod-strict, JSON) loaded by `aegis siege --roe path/to/roe.json` before engagement start. Schema captures operator authorization (org, authorized_by, contact, ≥20-char attestation), in/out scope, asset criticality, temporal envelope, stop conditions. Pre-engagement schema validation + scope/temporal checks. Synthesized minimal RoE used as back-compat fallback when --roe is omitted, with a yellow operator-warning.
ssrf-checker enforces RFC 1918 / private-IP / link-local block-rules in code paths and at config-schema level.

RoE schema in_scope.domains accepts wildcard patterns (`*.example.com`) and `includeSubdomains: boolean`. validateTargetInScope matches host against in_scope domains/IP CIDRs and against out_of_scope deny-list (deny wins).
RoE schema temporal envelope (start/end/timezone + optional blackout windows). validateTemporalEnvelope enforces pre-engagement that current time is within envelope and outside any blackout window.

RoE schema asset_criticality field maps patterns to (critical|high|medium|low) classifications. getAssetCriticality returns the matched classification (first-match-wins) for the validateAction context. Surfaces in operator-visible siege output.

validateAction is the composite pre-action gate: validates temporal envelope first, then in/out-of-scope, then surfaces asset criticality. Called both at engagement start and per-phase in `aegis siege`.

siege orchestrator calls validateTemporalEnvelope after each of the 4 phases (recon → discovery → exploitation → reporting). Halt-on-expiry with explicit reason. Continuous-monitoring at the granularity of phase boundaries.
aegis.config.json excludePaths + per-target opt-out provide a hard deny list consumed by every scanner. RoE out_of_scope (domains, ip_ranges, paths) extends this to siege-mode engagements.

siege emits a scope-validation event at engagement start (target + action + allowed + reason + apts_refs) into the same hash-chained JSONL audit log as the rest of the engagement events. Tamper-evident via aegis audit-verify <state-file>: any post-hoc alteration of the scope-decision is caught.
CWE-based severity (BLOCKER/HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW) is CIA-adjacent; no explicit CIA impact-vector scoring per finding.
Wrappers (Strix/PTAI/Pentest-Swarm-AI) accept their own rate-limit flags; orchestrator-side has no global rate-limit / payload-size envelope.
Ctrl+C terminates the process tree; no formal multi-path kill switch (signal-based, API-based, dead-man-switch).
No health-monitoring or threshold-based automatic halt today.
No post-test system-integrity validation step.
Mode-gate (--mode pentest only for LLM wrappers) is an external allowlist of which scanners run; no fine-grained per-action allowlist external to the model.
--mode pentest opt-in + siege --confirm are pre-approval gestures for L1/L2 wrappers; no structured authority-matrix per autonomy level.

siege --state-file emits a JSONL engagement event-stream (engagement-start, phase-transition enter/exit, finding-emitted, critical-finding, intervention, halt, kill, completion). Operator can tail the file for real-time visibility. Intervention surface: signal handlers (SIGUSR1 → graceful pause + state-dump; SIGTERM/SIGINT → kill + state-dump).
No decision-timeout / default-safe-behavior framework; wrappers run to completion.
No formal authority-delegation matrix.

SIGUSR1 handler dumps engagement state (completed_phases + findings_so_far + roe_id) to --state-file and exits 0. Operator resumes via --resume <path>; siege skips already-completed phases. State schema is Zod-strict.
No mid-engagement redirect capability — operator cannot point an in-flight siege at a different target without restart. Pause-and-edit-RoE-and-resume is partial workaround, not formal redirect.

SIGTERM/SIGINT handlers dump engagement state to --state-file (when configured) and emit a kill event with the dump-path before exit (143 / 130). State-dump is best-effort: a dump-write failure never masks the exit.
siege --confirm is a single mandatory decision point at engagement start; not per-irreversible-action.
No structured unexpected-findings escalation framework.
No impact-threshold-breach escalation; wrappers continue regardless of finding severity.
Findings carry confidence (high/medium/low) and the [LOW-CONFIDENCE] CI-badge is a low-fidelity escalation signal; no in-engagement confidence-based pause/escalation.
No legal/compliance escalation triggers.

siege --notify-webhook <url> dispatches high-signal events (engagement-start, critical-finding, halt, kill, completion) to one or more configured webhooks; multiple webhooks supported simultaneously. Full multi-channel coverage (Slack/email/PagerDuty native integrations) is Cluster-2.5 work. Per-engagement timeout, abort-on-fail-or-timeout, fire-and-forget shape (failures recorded as halt-events with non-fatal flag, never abort the engagement).
Wrappers execute one technique-per-invocation; orchestrator does not label scanner runs with formal AL-level tags.
Operator passes --target URL and selects scanner mode (scan/audit/siege/...) via CLI; technique selection is human-driven by construction.
aegis.config.json is fully operator-authored with strict Zod validation; no parameters are model-chosen at L1.
siege has 4 chained phases inside one engagement; operator opt-in via --confirm gates the chain at engagement start, not phase-by-phase.

Hash-chained JSONL audit log shipped via siege --state-file. Each event carries prev_hash + this_hash (SHA-256 over canonicalized JSON form). aegis audit-verify <state-file> validates the chain end-to-end. Per-finding evidence_hash on every finding-emitted event ties the audit trail to the underlying finding content.

RoE schema is the policy DSL: in_scope/out_of_scope domains + IP CIDRs + repository_paths, asset_criticality classifications, temporal envelope, stop_conditions. validateTargetInScope + validateAction enforce the policy at engagement start and per-phase.

Signal-based intervention (SIGUSR1 pause + SIGTERM/SIGINT kill) gives operators an out-of-band approval surface during runs. Per-action structured approval gates (RoE-driven, per-scanner-emit) are still net-new — that closes via the per-action validateAction expansion in Cluster-4.

RoE schema stop_conditions field declares the on_critical_finding policy (halt | notify-and-continue | continue) + max_findings + max_duration_minutes + on_target_unreachable_seconds. siege emits a critical-finding event for every blocker/critical/high finding with the operator-declared stop_action attached. Webhook dispatcher forwards to configured channels.

Multi-path kill switch: SIGTERM (signal-based, exit 143), SIGINT (Ctrl+C / TTY-driven, exit 130), and the kill-from-resume vector (operator pauses via SIGUSR1, edits RoE to mark engagement out-of-window, resumes — siege halts on temporal validation). State-dump on every termination path. Pause via SIGUSR1 + resume via --resume.

RoE schema is the multi-axis boundary-definition framework: in/out scope (domains, IP ranges, paths), temporal envelope (start/end + blackout windows), asset criticality, stop conditions, autonomy-level support is implicit in mode-gating. Enforcement via validateAction per-phase.
No continuous boundary-monitoring or breach-detection during runs.
Findings emit as structured JSON conforming to the AEGIS Finding schema and as SARIF 2.1.0 via packages/reporters/src/sarif.ts.

siege --state-file emits structured phase-transition events (enter/exit per phase + duration_ms) plus engagement-start, intervention, halt, kill, completion — all carrying ts (ISO-8601), engagement_id, event-name. The JSONL channel is the formal state-transition log demanded by AR-002.
Each finding carries an explicit confidence field (high/medium/low) wired to per-CWE confidence rules + scanner-specific confidence-downgrade logic.
Taint chains emit via Finding.relatedLocations + evidence field for SAST; siege-mode does not yet emit alternative-evaluation reasoning.

findingEvent computes a SHA-256 evidence_hash over the canonical form of every Finding and embeds it on the finding-emitted JSONL event. hashCanonical uses sorted-key recursive JSON canonicalization to ensure equal findings always produce equal hashes, regardless of property declaration order. RFC 6234 / FIPS 180-4 compliant via Node `crypto`.

ChainedEmitter wraps each emitted event with prev_hash (hash of the immediately-preceding event, null on first) + this_hash (SHA-256 of this event's canonical form, this_hash field excluded from preimage). Result: the JSONL audit log is a Merkle-style hash chain. Any single-line edit, reorder, or deletion breaks verification at the affected line. `aegis audit-verify <state-file>` walks the chain and reports broken_at line + reason.
No formal evidence-classification or sensitive-data-handling policy on emitted artifacts.
Each wrapper enforces its own instruction-boundary internally; AEGIS orchestrator has no own enforcement layer.
Wrappers parse their own outputs (e.g., PTAI's SARIF parser); orchestrator does not validate / sanitize wrapper responses beyond JSON-schema conformance.
error-leakage-checker enforces error-neutrality on the scanned-app side; AEGIS itself emits sanitized error messages without stack-traces in JSON output.
aegis.config.json is read at startup; no integrity-verification (hash-pin / signature) at runtime.
No authority-claim detection / rejection layer in the orchestrator.
open-redirect-checker exists at SAST level; orchestrator has no own redirect-following policy.
ssrf-checker covers SSRF/redirect prevention at SAST level; live DAST/siege relies on each wrapper's internal controls.
Same coverage as MR-008; no additional orchestrator-side SSRF prevention during testing operations.
No scope-expansion-social-engineering prevention at orchestrator level.
No out-of-band-communication prevention layer.
aegis.config.json is read once per run and the resulting scope is immutable for that run; not architecturally enforced (operator could mutate file mid-run, no detection).
Wrapper LLM I/O is not architecturally sandboxed beyond what each wrapper does internally.

next-public-leak, entropy-scanner, crypto-auditor, and jwt-detector form a multi-layer credential-protection scanner family covering NEXT_PUBLIC_ leakage, high-entropy strings, hardcoded secrets, and JWT-format credentials.
Each wrapper implements isAvailable() with double-gating (binary present + LLM API-key present); orchestrator skips unvetted wrappers with a [LOW-CONFIDENCE] note.
Operator manages API-key environment variables; AEGIS does not mediate authentication or rotate keys.
No formal incident-response / breach-notification workflow for provider-side compromise.
supply-chain scanner + dep-confusion-checker + lockfile integrity checks + monorepo-aware phantom-dep detection cover dependency inventory + supply-chain risk assessment.
config-auditor (Docker/Next.js/Firebase misconfig), header-checker (CSP/HSTS/COOP), and the ci/github-action's pinned-SHA + npm-install hardening cover cloud-config baseline.

Under the self-hosted-by-consumer deployment model, AEGIS itself does not ingest or persist client data; the operator runs AEGIS locally against their own code. Client-data classification is the operator's responsibility, not AEGIS's.
entropy-scanner + next-public-leak + jwt-detector discover sensitive-data-shaped patterns in source; no broader sensitive-data classification framework (PII/PHI/PCI categories).
crypto-auditor flags weak algorithms / insecure RNG / hardcoded secrets at SAST level; no orchestrator-side enforcement of in-flight encryption for AEGIS↔wrapper traffic.
Self-hosted-by-consumer model: AEGIS-side has no tenancy. Operator-side breach notification is the operator's responsibility under their own data-protection regime.
FOUNDATION-MODEL-DISCLOSURE.md documents the BYOM matrix per wrapper + the fix-mode companion; capability-baseline references point to provider model cards at the configured version.
[LOW-CONFIDENCE] PR-comment badge + per-CWE confidence rules + per-finding confidence field provide structured FP-rate disclosure on every report.
README scanner inventory + getAllScanners() + getAttackScanners() public registry expose vulnerability coverage; CHANGELOG documents per-release scanner additions.
Every report ends with a 0-1000 score + grade (FORTRESS/HARDENED/SOLID/...) + badge that serves as the executive-summary risk overview.
